New: Endpoint-only connector setup for Claude and ChatGPT with one MCP URL
OAuth 2.1 Dynamic Client Registration and PKCE flow backed by league/oauth2-server
OAuth discovery endpoints for protected resource and authorization server metadata
Custom OAuth tables for clients, authorization codes, access tokens, refresh tokens, and active sessions
Connector-neutral architecture for OAuth, MCP, Claude, and ChatGPT provider modules
OAuth authorization, consent, token exchange, refresh, and session revocation flow
Quark admin settings app with About, Connectors, Changelog, and Advanced tabs
Advanced option to remove plugin data on uninstall
Configurable AI Abilities tab for enabling or disabling MCP tools after a site is connected
Improved: Settings UI migrated to class-based markup with dedicated stylesheet
Connector setup experience redesigned around one MCP endpoint and provider cards
Field-level copy actions for the MCP endpoint and Claude command
POST nonce-based admin actions for improved reliability over nonce links
Streamable HTTP MCP endpoint behavior with SSE negotiation support
Resource-scoped OAuth metadata support for assistant connector setup
OAuth approval callback and token exchange resilience for assistant connector setup
Early well-known metadata handling to avoid WordPress canonical redirects serving HTML to ChatGPT
More tolerant dynamic client registration rate limit for assistant OAuth retry behavior
OpenAI docs-aligned OAuth metadata, PKCE enforcement, resource-scoped setup values, and MCP auth challenges
ChatGPT discovery compatibility by advertising the MCP endpoint as the primary authorization server issuer while preserving site-root metadata fallback
Dynamic Client Registration retry tolerance to avoid ChatGPT setup failures from repeated registration attempts
OAuth login return flow now lands on the Quark admin consent screen instead of resuming through the REST authorize URL
Dynamic Client Registration no longer returns plugin-level 429 responses for valid assistant registration attempts
MCP content and taxonomy tools now support read, create, and update operations across supported custom post types and custom taxonomies
Dev: Added prerelease GitHub Action to build Composer and npm artifacts on release
Added AGENTS.md standards for planning-first workflow and release discipline
